museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: January 2013: The year opens under next-generation rumours.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for January 2013. Regional timing and UK retail context should be checked before verification.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: Five researched month markers or context notes.

items.title: The year opens under next-generation rumours.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Tomb Raider
items.body: Lara Croft is rebooted as survival spectacle.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2013.

items.title: BioShock Infinite
items.body: Irrational trades Rapture for Columbia and fills the year with argument.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2013.

items.title: The Last of Us
items.body: Naughty Dog makes late-generation prestige feel bleak, intimate and technical.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for June 2013.

items.title: Papers, Please
items.body: A border booth becomes one of indie gaming's sharpest moral machines.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for August 2013.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto V
items.body: Rockstar makes Los Santos into a late-generation cultural event.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for September 17, 2013.

items.title: Pokemon X and Y
items.body: Pokemon moves into full 3D worldwide and all at once.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for October 2013.

items.title: Call of Duty: Ghosts
items.platform: PC / PS3 / PS4 / Xbox 360 / Xbox One
items.body: The annual shooter straddles old and new hardware.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2013.

items.title: The Legend of Zelda: A Link Between Worlds
items.body: Nintendo revisits a classic map with unusual lightness.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2013.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PlayStation 4 launches in Europe
items.body: Sony's console reaches Europe and the UK on November 29 with a strong price-and-power message.
items.sourceNote: Sony Interactive release confirms November 29, 2013.

items.title: Xbox One launches in the UK
items.body: Microsoft's console reaches the UK on November 22 after a difficult messaging year.
items.sourceNote: Xbox Wire confirms November 22, 2013 in initial markets including the UK.

items.title: Wii U struggles for attention
items.body: Nintendo's home console receives excellent games but remains commercially awkward.
items.sourceNote: 2013 Wii U context; verify specific sales claims separately.

items.title: Sharing becomes platform design
items.body: PS4's share button and Xbox recording features make broadcasting part of the console pitch.
items.sourceNote: 2013 platform context; verify feature details before final copy.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: GTA Online turns a boxed blockbuster into a service that stumbles, recovers and persists.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: Console reveals are watched, clipped and argued about instantly across Twitter, forums and YouTube.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: PS4 and Xbox One build sharing and recording into their identity.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Indie PC games such as Papers, Please prove small games can carry huge cultural weight online.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the end of one generation and the messy birth of another, with both happening in public.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The Last of Us and GTA V made PS3 and Xbox 360 seem almost defiant, as if they refused to leave quietly.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: PS4 and Xbox One arrived as arguments first and machines second: price, DRM, Kinect, sharing, used games, power.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: In the UK, November had proper launch-night electricity again, even if many players spent it refreshing stock pages.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
